revisionSummary: This revision packet identifies 3 area(s) of "INDEPENDENT CONTRACTOR AGREEMENT" warranting negotiation or revision, including 2 high-priority item(s). All suggested language is for review by a qualified professional only and does not constitute legal advice.
priorityChanges: Address: Unilateral Modification Rights — Request a mutual written amendment requirement.
Address: Limited Liability Cap — Negotiate a higher cap and carve-outs for willful misconduct.
Add missing clauses: Force majeure clause (not clearly present), Data processing agreement / GDPR addendum, SLA / uptime guarantees

clauseRevisions.originalLanguage: IN NO EVENT SHALL COMPANY BE LIABLE TO CONTRACTOR FOR ANY INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES ARISING OUT OF OR RELATED TO THIS AGREEMENT
clauseRevisions.issue: Liability cap may be significantly below potential damages
clauseRevisions.suggestedReplacementLanguage: [SUGGESTED FOR PROFESSIONAL REVIEW] Consider negotiating a higher cap, or carve-outs for willful misconduct, gross negligence, and data breach scenarios.
clauseRevisions.whyItMatters: Low liability caps leave you with no meaningful recourse for serious failures.
clauseRevisions.supportingQuote: IN NO EVENT SHALL COMPANY BE LIABLE TO CONTRACTOR FOR ANY INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES ARISING OUT OF OR RELATED TO THIS AGREEMENT

clauseRevisions.originalLanguage: Renewal clause language not extracted from document text.
clauseRevisions.issue: Auto-renewal with potentially short cancellation window
clauseRevisions.suggestedReplacementLanguage: [SUGGESTED FOR PROFESSIONAL REVIEW] Negotiate a shorter notice window (e.g., 30 days) and require affirmative renewal consent.
clauseRevisions.whyItMatters: Missing the cancellation window locks you into another full-term commitment.
clauseRevisions.supportingQuote: Renewal or term clause not identified in parsed text. Review document for renewal and termination provisions.

negotiationNotes: Start with the highest-severity items first
Document all verbal agreements in writing before signing
Request a redline of any counter-proposals
Consider requesting a 30-day review period before signing
Get all changes confirmed in a written amendment or restated agreement
lawyerQuestions: Is the liability cap sufficient for our potential exposure?
Are the indemnification obligations reasonable and balanced?
What is the practical impact of the governing law choice?
What are our rights and remedies if the other party materially breaches?
Is the IP assignment clause broad enough to cover all work product?
Is the non-compete enforceable in our jurisdiction and scope?
revisionDisclaimer: Suggested revisions are not legal advice. They are suggested replacement language for review by a qualified professional. Consult an attorney before using any suggested language.
